column1: # Modern Esperanto root supplement — curated, human-authored.
# Genuine modern borrowings (acronyms, recent technology terms, internet-era
# vocabulary) are absent from ESPDIC and would otherwise show up as
# unresolved by the decomposer. This file is the project's curated intake
# path: every entry is human-vetted, NOT auto-mined.
# Loaded by src/lexicon/build_eo_inventory.py and merged into the inventory
# under tier "modern" (treated like "extended" by the decomposer for the
# keep-whole / selection rules). A supplement entry NEVER overwrites an
# existing ESPDIC root of the same key — duplicate entries are silently
# ignored.
# Format (tab-separated): root <TAB> gloss <TAB> note (provenance / date /
# rationale for the addition). Blank lines and lines starting with "#" are
# ignored.
# Do NOT add ambiguous truncations like `klim` (for klimato) or `apar` (for
# aparato) — those are upstream extraction bugs, not modern borrowings.
# Reduction-class false merges (e.g. prezid being collapsed into prez) go
# into eo_reduce_exceptions.txt, not here.
# License of this file: project data, human-authored. The glosses are short
# factual definitions; no third-party license entanglement.
